message:
fix(serve): propagate auth claims through middleware and pick highest realm_access role

Two related RBAC bugs: (1) OIDC-authenticated requests validated a
token but then threw the claims away, so downstream handlers
couldn't tell who the caller was or what role they had.
(2) When the caller's role came from a Keycloak realm_access.roles
array, the extractor returned on the *first* Admin/Operator it saw
and ignored the role hierarchy altogether.

internal/serve/server.go

- authenticateRequest(r) previously returned `error`.  Widen to
  `(map[string]interface{}, error)` so OIDC can hand the decoded
  claims back up to the middleware.  API-key and mTLS modes return
  `(nil, ...)` because they don't have a JWT to speak of.  Local
  mode now returns a synthetic claims map
  `{"role": string(RoleAdmin)}` so local dev gets admin privileges
  via the same code path the middleware uses — this replaces the
  old "if Local then skip auth entirely" short-circuit.
- authenticateOIDC: returns `(map[string]interface{}, error)`,
  delegating to validateOIDCToken.
- validateOIDCToken: parses the JWT as before, but now returns the
  claims map on success instead of just `nil`.  The helper is
  otherwise unchanged (iss/aud/exp/nbf/sig verification is the
  same).
- authMiddlewareFunc and authMiddleware (the chi and legacy
  variants): drop the `if Local { next } else { check }` shortcut,
  leaving only the OPTIONS preflight bypass.  On success they read
  the returned claims and, if non-nil, attach them to the request
  context via `context.WithValue(r.Context(), authContextKey,
  claims)`.  That makes the claims available to downstream
  handlers/RBAC without re-parsing the token.

internal/serve/rbac.go

- extractRoleFromClaims, realm_access.roles branch: iterate the
  whole array, track `maxRole` via roleHierarchy(), and return
  `maxRole` when it's above RoleViewer.  The old code returned on
  the first Admin seen, or the first Operator seen, which is only
  correct if the roles array happens to be sorted that way.  In
  practice Keycloak tokens list realm roles in alphabetical order
  of realm role names, so a user with `["admin","operator","viewer"]`
  used to resolve correctly but `["editor","operator","viewer"]`
  would skip straight past Operator looking for an exact Admin
  match and land on Viewer.  Using roleHierarchy() makes the
  "strongest wins" semantics explicit and order-independent.

Together these fix the regression where OIDC-authenticated callers
with correct realm roles were being treated as RoleViewer (or the
fallback) for every request, because (a) the claims never made it
to the RBAC lookup and (b) even if they had, the role parser was
dropping them.

No new tests in this commit — the existing auth middleware tests
still pass because Local mode's behavior (admin) is preserved, and
the OIDC path is covered by validateOIDCToken's unit tests. A
focused test for the Keycloak hierarchy fix should be added in a
follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
